title: cistus villosus subsp. corsicus
intro: Cistus villosus subsp. corsicus, also known as the Corsican hairy rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cistus villosus subsp. corsicus include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as acting as an antifungal and antibacterial agent. Cistus villosus subsp. corsicus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, and has been used to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
